minds.title: Infusion Pump Accuracy
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: The infusion pump shall deliver medication within ±5% of the programmed flow rate across the full operating range to prevent patient harm from over- or under-dosing.

type_specific_attributes.content: Infusion pump shall maintain flow rate accuracy within ±5% of programmed rate for rates between 0.1 mL/hr and 1200 mL/hr across all supported fluid viscosities.
type_specific_attributes.acceptance_criteria: Flow rate accuracy verified within ±5% across full range using gravimetric test method per IEC 60601-2-24.

minds.title: Biocompatibility of Patient Contact Materials
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: All materials in the fluid path that contact the patient or medication shall be biocompatible and shall not leach harmful substances under normal and foreseeable misuse conditions.

type_specific_attributes.content: Patient contact materials shall pass ISO 10993-5 cytotoxicity, ISO 10993-10 sensitization, and ISO 10993-11 systemic toxicity testing. Extractables and leachables shall be below established safety thresholds.
type_specific_attributes.acceptance_criteria: All patient contact materials pass ISO 10993 biological evaluation with no adverse findings.

minds.title: Mitigation for: Dose Delivery Error
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Implement closed-loop flow rate control with upstream and downstream pressure sensors. Add audible and visual alarms for flow rate deviation exceeding ±10%.

minds.title: Dose Delivery Error
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Patient receives an incorrect medication dose due to flow rate deviation, potentially causing therapeutic failure or toxic overdose.

minds.title: Mitigation for: Software Malfunction Leading to Unsafe Output
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Develop software per IEC 62304 Class C lifecycle. Implement independent hardware watchdog and redundant flow monitoring. Conduct static analysis and formal code review.

minds.title: Software Malfunction Leading to Unsafe Output
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Software defect or runtime error causes the pump to deliver an unintended flow rate or fail to stop infusion when required.

minds.title: Mitigation for: Allergic Reaction to Patient Contact Material
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Use medical-grade silicone and DEHP-free PVC for all fluid-path components. Conduct ISO 10993-10 sensitization testing on all patient contact materials.

minds.title: Allergic Reaction to Patient Contact Material
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Patient experiences local or systemic allergic reaction due to sensitizing substances released from tubing, connectors, or other fluid-path components.

minds.title: Mitigation for: Toxic Substance Leaching into Fluid Path
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Perform extractables and leachables study per ISO 10993-18. Select materials with established safety profiles for prolonged contact applications.

minds.title: Toxic Substance Leaching into Fluid Path
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Harmful chemical substances leach from fluid-path materials into the medication, causing systemic toxicity in the patient over prolonged infusion periods.

minds.title: Pump Mechanism Wear
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Progressive mechanical wear of the peristaltic pump rotor and tubing compression mechanism causes flow rate drift over the device service life.

minds.title: Flow Sensor Drift
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Ultrasonic or drop-counting flow sensor measurement drifts from calibration, providing inaccurate flow rate feedback to the control system.

minds.title: Firmware Calculation Error
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Arithmetic error in the dose calculation firmware module produces an incorrect motor drive signal, causing the pump to deliver the wrong flow rate.

minds.title: Stepper Motor Stall
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Peristaltic pump stepper motor loses synchronization and stalls, causing intermittent or complete cessation of fluid delivery.

minds.title: Silicone Tubing Degradation
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Medical-grade silicone tubing in the fluid path degrades due to repeated sterilization cycles or chemical interaction with aggressive medications.

minds.title: Plasticizer Leaching from PVC Components
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: DEHP or other plasticizers leach from PVC connectors or tubing segments into the medication fluid, particularly with lipid-containing solutions.

minds.title: Sterilization Residue on Fluid Path Components
minds.creator: fmea-iso14971@example.com
minds.description: Ethylene oxide or other sterilization agent residues remain on fluid-path components above acceptable limits after aeration, causing irritation or toxicity.
